requirements_for_qualification.summary: Must analyze and select optimal pass-through entity structure (sole proprietorship, partnership, LLC, S Corp) based on liability protection, tax efficiency, administrative burden, and business objectives
criteria: Consider liability protection needs, operational flexibility, and management structure requirements
Analyze self-employment tax implications for each entity type and owner compensation strategies
Evaluate qualified business income (QBI) deduction optimization across different entity structures
Compare administrative compliance burden including state fees, filing requirements, and ongoing maintenance costs
Consider owner basis calculations, loss limitations, and tax attribute tracking requirements
Assess flexibility for future growth, multiple owners, equity compensation, and exit strategies
Evaluate state tax treatment as many states impose entity-level taxes or don't conform to federal classification
Plan for entity conversion or restructuring timing to minimize tax costs and maximize strategic benefits

entity_type_comparison.sole_proprietorship: Simplest form; Schedule C reporting; full self-employment tax; no liability protection; QBI deduction available
entity_type_comparison.partnership_llc: Multi-member default; K-1 reporting; self-employment tax on guaranteed payments and distributive share; flexible allocations
entity_type_comparison.single_member_llc: Disregarded entity default; Schedule C reporting; can elect S Corp or C Corp status; state law liability protection
entity_type_comparison.s_corporation: Payroll required; reasonable compensation rules; distribution optimization; limited to 100 shareholders; one class of stock
entity_type_comparison.self_employment_tax: 15.3% on sole prop/partnership income; S Corp distributions avoid SE tax; balance with reasonable compensation
entity_type_comparison.qbi_deduction_interaction: Available for all pass-through types; W-2 wages and property basis affect phase-out calculations

restrictions: Entity classification elections (Form 8832) and S Corp elections (Form 2553) have strict timing requirements
Conversion between entity types may trigger deemed sale, recognition of income, or basis adjustments
Self-employment tax applies to general partners and LLC members for guaranteed payments and distributive share
S Corporation restrictions limit ownership flexibility and capital structure compared to partnerships
Partnership allocations must have substantial economic effect; special allocation rules complex and require careful planning
State entity-level taxes (PTET) in some states allow workaround for $10,000 SALT deduction limitation
Basis limitations restrict pass-through loss deductions; at-risk and passive activity rules layer additional restrictions
Professional service businesses face additional QBI deduction phase-outs and limitations requiring careful entity planning
